  • Uses of LoginController in view

    Constructors in view with parameters of type LoginController
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    ChangePasswordAdminWindow(ListAdminWindow listAdminWindow, LoginController cont, String adminName)
    Creates a new dialog to change the administrator's password.
     
    FormAdminWindow(JDialog adminListWindow, LoginController controller, Administrator administrator, boolean isInsert)
    Constructs the form dialog for adding or editing an administrator.
     
    FormBookWindow(JDialog parentWindow, LoginController controller, Book booking, boolean isInsert)
    Constructs the booking form dialog.
     
    FormClientWindow(JDialog clientManagement, LoginController controller, Client client, boolean isInsert)
    Constructs a new dialog for client management (creation or modification).
     
    FormCruiseWindow(JDialog cruiseListWindow, LoginController controller, Cruise cruise, boolean isInsert)
    Constructs the cruise form dialog window.
     
    FormWorkerWindow(ListWorkerWindow workerListWindow, LoginController cont, Worker worker, boolean b)
    Constructs the worker form dialog window.
     
    ListAdminWindow(JFrame mainWindow, LoginController cont)
    Constructs the administrator list window.
     
    ListBookWindow(JFrame mainWindow, LoginController cont)
    Constructs the list booking dialog.
     
    ListClientWindow(JFrame mainWindow, LoginController cont)
    Constructs the window showing all clients.
     
    ListCruiseWindow(JFrame mainWindow, LoginController cont)
    Constructs the list cruises window dialog.
     
    ListWorkerWindow(MainWindow mainWindow, LoginController cont)
    Creates the dialog for listing and managing workers.
     
    LoginWindow(StartWindow startWindow, LoginController cont)
    Constructs the login graphical dialog.
     
    MainWindow(LoginWindow loginWindow, LoginController cont)
    Initializes the main dashboard interface window.
     
    StartWindow(LoginController loginController)
    Launches the startup window setting up graphical dependencies.
